text:
Once upon a time, in a cozy town filled with laughter and sunflowers, there lived a wise old fox named Felix. Felix was renowned not only for his sharp eyes and cunning mind but also for his love of storytelling. One quiet autumn afternoon, while munching slowly on an apple, he heard the faint sound of children’s laughter echoing through the leaves.

Curious, Felix set off, his belly jiggling with every step, towards the source of the sound. After some searching, he stumbled upon a little playground, surrounded by a tapestry of autumn colors. Children were running here and there, their laughter ringing through the air, creating patterns of light and shadow in the falling leaves.

Among them was a spirited young girl with shimmering hazel eyes and an infectious giggle. Her name was Elodie. Seeing the wise fox, she stopped, her eyes twinkling mischievously.

“Hey! Who are you?” Elodie asked with a grin.

“I’m Felix
